texto: Ultimately, musicians of the world must come realise the potential of their calling.Like the shamans, we may serve as healers, metaphysicians, inciters, exciters,spiritual guides and sources of inspiration. If the musician is illuminated from within,he becomes a lamp that lights other lamps. Then he is serving planet and its people,healing what ails us. Such music is truly important. It is said that “only one who obeys can truly command.” When the artist is immersed in a services,giving himself up over and over again, another paradox occurs:He is being seen by all others as a master.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kenny Werner: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a motivacao. O trecho “Ultimately, musicians of the world must come realise the potential of their calling.Like the sham…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, art, artistry pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e artistry.

If the musician is illuminated from within,he becomes a lamp that lights other lamps. Then he is serving planet and its people,healing what ails us. Such music is truly important. It is said that “only one who obeys can truly command.” When the artist is immersed in a services,giving himself up over and over again, another paradox occurs:He is being seen by all others as a master.

texto: We are all part of a universal game. Returning to our essence while living in the world is the object of the game. The earth is the game board, and we are the pieces on the board. We move around and around until we remember who we really are, and then we can be taken off the board. At that point, we are no longer the game-piece, but the player; we've won the game.
explicacao: Citação de Kenny Werner sobre temas ligados a vida. “We are all part of a universal game. Returning to our essence while living in the world is the ob…” articula vida, mastery-of-oneself, philosophy-of-life em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como philosophy-of-life e zen.

texto: A quiet mind allows the artist to tap into the wellspring of Divine Music within.
